---
title: "AI Red-Team Loop"
---

# AI Red-Team Loop

**Working seal:** `stack://ai-red-team-loop/6CF8F7698D`

**Enter:** Use when an LLM-assisted workflow needs controlled adversarial testing for prompt injection, jailbreak pressure, leakage, or tool misuse.


## Related Runes

- [1054 threat model](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1054) - A structured view of likely attackers, assets, and failure paths.
- [1390 prompt injection](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1390) - `prompt injection` is a promptcraft and AI-assisted engineering rune for bind model behavior to role, context, output contract, and evidence; use it when the artifact needs the prompt injection obligation named before work proceeds.
- [1374 guardrail](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1374) - A boundary that narrows allowed behavior without fully specifying the answer.
- [1034 quarantine](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1034) - `quarantine` is a security and trust rune for make identity, authority, secrecy, and enforcement boundaries auditable; use it when the artifact needs the quarantine obligation named before work proceeds.
- [1042 sandbox {Sec}](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1042) - Constrain code inside a smaller, safer prison.
- [1017 least privilege](../reference/house-security-trust-and-warding-words.qmd#rune-1017) - Give only the authority required for the present act, no more. It is one of the simplest words for reducing future regret.
- [1364 evaluation {AI}](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1364) - The act of measuring whether the model is actually useful.
- [1414 tool call](../reference/house-promptcraft-ai-oriented-engineering-and-spell-structure.qmd#rune-1414) - A step where the model reaches beyond text into external action.


## Related Spell Templates and References

- [Jailbreak Resilience Review](../spells/jailbreak-resilience-review.qmd)
- [Bug Diagnosis From Logs](../spells/bug-diagnosis-from-logs.qmd)
- [Stack Grammar](../reference/stack-grammar.qmd)
- [Failure Modes](../reference/failure-modes.qmd)
- [Canon Map](../reference/canon-map.qmd)
| Step | Spell                 | Artifact                                                                      | Advance when                                                      |
| ---- | --------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------------------- | ----------------------------------------------------------------- |
| 1    | Threat model          | Assets, trust boundaries, allowed tools, untrusted inputs, and attacker goals | The security boundary and benign task are explicit                |
| 2    | Build defanged probes | Harmless fixture prompts and canary-bearing test inputs                       | Probes preserve attack shape without operational payloads         |
| 3    | Run in sandbox        | Prompt, transcript, fixture version, and tool-scope record                    | The model output and tool behavior are captured verbatim          |
| 4    | Score resistance      | Attack-resistance, utility-preservation, and audit-quality scores             | Scores cite the transcript and fixture expectations               |
| 5    | Mitigate              | Prompt, policy, retrieval, or tool-scope repair                               | The repair addresses the observed failure without blanket refusal |
| 6    | Replay                | Repeated run set across fixture versions or model surfaces                    | The failure is fixed or residual risk is accepted explicitly      |
| 7    | Report                | Defensive finding with source map, evidence, mitigation, and residual risk    | A maintainer can reproduce the finding without unsafe payloads    |

## Loop or Recursion

```json
{
  "steps": [
    2,
    3,
    4,
    5,
    6
  ],
  "until": "Attack resistance and utility preservation meet the release threshold, or residual risk is documented for human review"
}
```


## On Fail

Stop the test if a probe would require operational harmful content; replace it with a defanged skeleton and record the omitted class of detail.

## Why It Works

It treats adversarial promptcraft as evidence-producing security work instead of social-media prompt collecting.
